questions.question: Which tool is recommended for building fast, simple landing pages at the '$0/month Starter Stack' level?

options.text: Salesforce Marketing Cloud
Carrd
Webflow Enterprise
Marketo

questions.explanation: Carrd is the go-to recommendation for simple, one-page sites and waitlists due to its speed, simplicity, and low cost (Free to $19/year).

questions.question: Why does the lesson suggest using 'Native Integrations' over 'Glue' workarounds? (e.g., Zapier)

options.text: Native integrations are usually more expensive.
Native integrations are faster, more reliable, and have fewer failure points.
Most tools don't allow Zapier connections.
Zapier is only for enterprise companies.

questions.explanation: Built-in, native integrations between tools are more stable and easier to maintain than chaining tools together with external services like Zapier.

questions.question: A founder has $2k/mo in revenue and wants to save time by automating cross-tool data flow. Which addition to their stack is most appropriate according to the lesson?

options.text: Hiring a full-time marketing manager.
Zapier Starter or a similar integration tool.
Upgrading to HubSpot Enterprise.
Purchasing 10 different specialized Micro-SaaS tools.

questions.explanation: At the '$50/month Professional Stack' level, adding an integration tool like Zapier provides the 'glue' needed to automate data flow between separate systems.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of 'Consolidating' your tools when possible?

options.text: It ensures you have more logins to manage.
It keeps your data in separate silos for better security.
It simplifies management and provides a better 'Single Source of Truth' for lead data.
It increases the number of monthly subscriptions.

questions.explanation: Consolidation into an all-in-one tool or a tight-knit ecosystem reduces complexity and gives you a unified view of your leads.

questions.question: What is 'Subscription Creep' and how do you prevent it?

options.text: When your traffic increases too fast; prevent by slowing marketing.
The slow accumulation of unused paid tools; prevent by auditing every 6 months.
When customers unsubsidize from your newsletter; prevent by removing the 'Unsubscribe' button.
The cost of hiring new employees.

questions.explanation: Subscription creep is the buildup of recurring costs for tools you no longer use. Regular audits and cancellations help keep your bootstrap stack lean.

questions.question: For a Digital Creator selling courses globally, why might they choose a 'Merchant of Record' (like LemonSqueezy) over a standard payment processor (like Stripe)?

options.text: It is cheaper.
It handles global sales tax (VAT) compliance automatically, which is manually impossible for a solo founder.
It has better colors.
It allows you to accept crypto only.

questions.explanation: Selling digital goods globally triggers VAT tax nexus in dozens of countries. A Merchant of Record (MoR) like LemonSqueezy acts as the reseller, handling all tax liability for you.

questions.question: Identify ONE tool in your current stack (or one you are considering) that you aren't fully using or could be consolidated. Research if your 'Base' tool (CRM or Email) has a native feature that could replace it. If you were to rebuild your stack today on a $50/month budget, what would be your 3 core tools and why?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's consolidation logic and budget-conscious stack design. Check for redundant tools in their proposed $50 stack. Suggest a specific free trial or tier they can start with for one of their chosen tools.
